Infoblox Confidential page ii 1.0 Overview Due to the widespread concern regarding the recently publicized COVID-19 coronavirus (“coronavirus”), Infoblox has prepared the following pandemic policy and plan (“plan”). In the event of a pandemic associated with coronavirus, Infoblox intends to implement the following plan with the goals of protecting the health of its employees, minimizing disturbance to its customers, and continuing the vitality of its business. 2.0 Process Section 2.1 Scope This policy applies to all Infoblox employees and contractors. 2.2 Process Narrative Spread of Flu In general, respiratory viruses (including the coronavirus) spread easily from person to person through infected respiratory droplets being circulated in the air through general breathing, coughing, or sneezing into the air. Such droplets remain airborne for a relatively short time and then settle on surfaces.

Such droplets remain airborne for a relatively short time and then settle on surfaces. People become infected by inhaling these droplets or by touching contaminated surfaces and then touching their mouth or nose. As with seasonal influenza, infected persons are able to transmit the coronavirus before they exhibit any symptoms themselves. Heath officials estimate that the coronavirus can be transmitted for up to fourteen (14) days after exposure to the coronavirus even if the individual doesn’t experience any symptoms, making the coronavirus potentially more challenging to contain than the seasonal flu. Also, since the coronavirus is new and facts have been hard to verify, the particular human-to-human transmission vectors are still being evaluated by public health officials worldwide. Notwithstanding this, this plan summarizes the consensus of how to combat a new and emerging threat.

Notwithstanding this, this plan summarizes the consensus of how to combat a new and emerging threat. With this in mind, we recommend and encourage employees to be vigilant in washing and sanitizing their hands in order to minimize the spread of the coronavirus. This plan also states that employees will: 1. Work remotely and will not come to work in the office (and will leave work immediately) if they have been running a fever, if they have a sore throat or cough, if they have vomited, if they feel achy or lethargic, and if a member of the employee’s household has been running a fever. © Infoblox Inc. All rights reserved. Infoblox Confidential page 1 2. Not return to work in the office until 14 days after the initial onset of symptoms and they have been 24 hours fever-free and symptom-free. 3. Be encouraged to cough and sneeze into disposable tissues and immediately place used tissues in a waste basket, and if a tissue is not available, cough or sneeze into the elbow.

3. Be encouraged to cough and sneeze into disposable tissues and immediately place used tissues in a waste basket, and if a tissue is not available, cough or sneeze into the elbow. 4. Be encouraged to avoid touching their eyes, nose, mouth, or face and to frequently wash their hands, especially after using a tissue, the restroom, or helping someone who is sick. 5. Be encouraged to seek medical treatment and to work from home for 14 days after symptoms first develop if the employee is confirmed or suspected of having been infected by the coronavirus. Employees are required to notify their managers of any expected absence from the office in accordance with standard Infoblox policy. Travel When possible, employees will be encouraged to avoid travel and face-to-face meeting, and instead use alternatives, such as telephone or video conferencing.

Travel When possible, employees will be encouraged to avoid travel and face-to-face meeting, and instead use alternatives, such as telephone or video conferencing. Where travel for Infoblox business is necessary, employees will be required to comply with travel recommendations and restrictions issued by governmental agencies. Employees will be encouraged to consult the CDC website (currently at: http://www.cdc.gov/travel) or equivalent source for their locality, with up-to-date information and travel advisories and to visit their doctor to obtain recommended vaccines and medications. Business Continuity Considerations In addition to Infoblox’s headquarters in Santa Clara, CA and its Centers of Excellence in Tacoma, WA and Bangalore, India, Infoblox maintains operations at various locations around the world as part of its normal business. Customer Technical Support Resources: Santa Clara, CA; Tacoma, WA; Antwerp, Belgium; Trivandrum, India; Tokyo, Japan; Malaysia.

Customer Technical Support Resources: Santa Clara, CA; Tacoma, WA; Antwerp, Belgium; Trivandrum, India; Tokyo, Japan; Malaysia. All of the support centers have the capability to operate on a 24x7x365 schedule and are staffed locally with TAC and Escalation Support engineers. Engineering Resources: Santa Clara, CA; Tacoma, WA; Burnaby, BC, Canada; Minsk, Belarus RMA Replacement Resources: Singapore; Tokyo, Japan; Sydney, Australia; Hong Kong, China; Shanghai, China; Bangalore, India; Toronto, Canada; Amsterdam, Netherlands. If one of the depot locations is no longer able to perform its function, Infoblox can direct RMA stock from alternative © Infoblox Inc. All rights reserved. Infoblox Confidential page 2 locations.

If one of the depot locations is no longer able to perform its function, Infoblox can direct RMA stock from alternative © Infoblox Inc. All rights reserved. Infoblox Confidential page 2 locations. In the event of a pandemic coronavirus crisis affecting one or more locations, business functions and/or the availability of equipment, Infoblox will continue to manage and adjust resources and assignments at each location as possible with the goal of minimizing any disruption to customers’ businesses. Remote Access to Systems and Tools Through the use of information technology, employees are generally able to access Infoblox systems and work remotely from any location to participate in customer support, engineering, order processing and other activities. Key systems and tools are located in a secure collocation facility that can be accessed and maintained remotely. Most Infoblox employees have been issued laptop computers as part of their standard workstation equipment.

Most Infoblox employees have been issued laptop computers as part of their standard workstation equipment. Employees’ laptops already contain necessary software and configuration to be used remotely if the employee is required or requested to work remotely. If Infoblox management determines it is necessary or advisable for individuals to remain at home and/or minimize the people coming onsite in order to avoid exposure, Infoblox management will request each department head to categorize functions and personnel in two groups: (A) those functions/personnel critical to have onsite, and (B) personnel that can satisfactorily work remotely on a temporary basis. In such an event, Infoblox management may request some or all of the category B personnel to work from home during the crisis.

In such an event, Infoblox management may request some or all of the category B personnel to work from home during the crisis. Summary Infoblox believes that the measures described above are appropriate to protect the health and safety of Infoblox employees while sustaining the business of Infoblox and its customers through a pandemic crisis. Aspects of this Pandemic Policy and Plan may be implemented in whole or in part in the event of a crisis, as determined by Infoblox management. 2.3 Frequently Asked Questions None 2.4 Definitions None © Infoblox Inc.
